title: विष्णु भुजंग स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु भुजंग स्तोत्रम् ॥

चिदानन्दरूपं सदा विश्वमूर्तिं
सुरेशं परेशं नमामि प्रभुम्।
गदाशंखचक्रारविन्दं दधानं
परानन्दसन्दोहमीशं भजामि॥ १॥

किरीटोज्ज्वलत्कुण्डलाभ्यां विभातं
ललाटे तिलक्यश्रियं बिभ्रतं च।
मुखाम्भोजमिन्दुप्रकाशं हसन्तं
सदा भावयामि प्रभुं विश्वनाथम्॥ २॥

भुजंगप्रयातप्रबन्धैरुदारैः
स्तवं वै करोमि प्रभोः केशवस्य।
प्रसन्नो भवत्वच्युतो मेऽभिलाषं
ददातु प्रसादं सदा माधवो मे॥ ३॥

जगत्सृष्टिसंहारकारं महान्तं
स्थितिं पालनं रक्षणं कुर्वतं च।
अजं शाश्वतं कारणं कारणानां
भजे विष्णुमव्यक्तरूपं परेशम्॥ ४॥

वराहं नृसिंहं च वामनरूपं
मत्स्यं कूर्ममत्यद्भुतं रामचन्द्रम्।
परश्वन्थधारिणमीशं बुद्धं
कल्किं चावतारं भजे विश्वमूर्तिम्॥ ५॥

सुरद्रुद्रुमं कामधेनुं सुरत्नं
चिन्तामणिं सर्वसम्पत्प्रदं च।
त्वमेवासि सर्वस्य कर्ता विधाता
नमस्ते सदा श्रीपतेऽनन्तमूर्ते॥ ६॥

इदं भुजंगप्रयाताभिधानं
स्तवं विष्णोरतिपावनं यः पठेत्।
स विष्णोः परं धाम गच्छेत्
प्रमुच्य भवबन्धनात् सर्वपापात्॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु भुजंग स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Bhujanga Stotram ||

Chidananda-rupam sada vishva-murtim
Suresham paresham namami prabhum |
Gada-shankha-chakra-aravindam dadhanam
Parananda-sandoham-isham bhajami || 1 ||

Kiritojjvalat-kundalabhyam vibhatam
Lalate tilakya-shriyam bibhratam cha |
Mukhambhojam-indu-prakasham hasantam
Sada bhavayami prabhum vishvanatham || 2 ||

Bhujanga-prayata-prabandhair-udaraih
Stavam vai karomi prabhoh Keshavasya |
Prasanno bhavat-vachyuto me'bhilasham
Dadatu prasadam sada Madhavo me || 3 ||

Jagat-srishti-samhara-karam mahantam
Sthitim palanam rakshanam kurvatam cha |
Ajam shashvatam karanam karananam
Bhaje Vishnum-avyakta-rupam paresham || 4 ||

Varaham Nrisimham cha Vamana-rupam
Matsyam Kurmam-atyadbhutam Ramachandram |
Parashvantha-dharinam-isham Buddham
Kalkim chavataram bhaje vishva-murtim || 5 ||

Sura-dru-drumam kamadhemum suratnam
Chintamanim sarva-sampat-pradam cha |
Tvam-evasi sarvasya karta vidhata
Namaste sada Shripate'nanta-murte || 6 ||

Idam bhujanga-prayatabhidhanam
Stavam Vishnor-ati-pavanam yah pathet |
Sa Vishnoh param dhama gachchhet
Pramuchya bhava-bandhanat sarva-papat || 7 ||

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Bhujanga Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
चिदानन्द स्वरूप, सदा विश्वमूर्ति, सुरों और परमेश के ईश्वर प्रभु को नमन करता हूँ। गदा, शंख, चक्र और पद्म धारण करने वाले, परमानन्द के भण्डार ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ १॥

किरीट से उज्ज्वल, कुण्डलों से शोभित, ललाट पर तिलक की शोभा धारण करते हुए, चन्द्र प्रकाश मुखकमल से मुस्कुराते हुए विश्वनाथ प्रभु का सदा चिन्तन करता हूँ॥ २॥

भुजंगप्रयात छन्द के उदार बन्ध से केशव प्रभु की स्तुति करता हूँ। अच्युत प्रसन्न हों, माधव मेरी अभिलाषा पूरी करें, सदा प्रसाद प्रदान करें॥ ३॥

जगत की सृष्टि और संहार करने वाले महान, स्थिति-पालन-रक्षण करते हुए, अज, शाश्वत, कारणों के कारण, अव्यक्त रूप, परमेश विष्णु को भजता हूँ॥ ४॥

वराह, नृसिंह, वामन, मत्स्य, कूर्म, अत्यद्भुत रामचन्द्र, परशु धारी, बुद्ध और कल्कि अवतार वाले विश्वमूर्ति को भजता हूँ॥ ५॥

आप ही कल्पवृक्ष, कामधेनु, चिन्तामणि और सर्व सम्पत्ति प्रदान करने वाले हैं। आप ही सबके कर्ता और विधाता हैं। अनन्तमूर्ति श्रीपति को सदा नमस्कार॥ ६॥

जो इस भुजंगप्रयात नामक अतिपावन विष्णु स्तव का पाठ करता है, वह सभी पापों और भवबन्धन से मुक्त होकर विष्णु के परम धाम को प्राप्त करता है॥ ७॥
benefits: Composed in the elegant Bhujanga Prayata metre for melodious recitation
Invokes the blessing of divine grace of lord vishnu
Frees the devotee from the cycle of birth and death
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants liberation
Traditionally associated with peace and spiritual elevation
description: Vishnu Bhujanga Stotram — विष्णु भुजंग स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Vishnu. Attributed to Traditional Vaishnavite Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to composed in the elegant Bhujanga Prayata metre for melodious recitation and bestow divine grace of Lord Vishnu. It is also said to destroy all sins and grant liberation. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ekadashi. The text contains 7 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Bhujanga Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Bhujanga Stotram in Bhujanga Prayata metre - Sanskrit text with transliteration and Hindi meaning praising Lord Vishnu.
relatedSearches: Vishnu Bhujanga Prayata Stotram lyrics Sanskrit
Shiva Bhujanga Prayata Stotram Shankara mother
Vishnu Bhujangam benefits at death moksha

seoFaqs.question: What is the prosodic meter Bhujanga Prayata and why is it used?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhujanga Prayata is a Sanskrit chandas (meter) consisting of four padas (lines) of twelve syllables each, with the syllabic pattern y-y-y-y (yagana repeated four times: laghu-guru-guru). The literal translation is the gliding of a serpent, since the rhythmic flow mimics the sinuous movement of a snake. Adi Shankaracharya found this meter particularly suited to praise hymns because its three short heavy beats per foot create a forward-flowing momentum that carries the devotee through long sequences of divine epithets without monotony. He composed Bhujanga stotras for Vishnu, Shiva, Devi, Subramanya, Sharada and Ganesha, all using the same meter. (Sources: Hindupedia Vishnu Bhujanga Prayata; Greenmesg Subramanya Bhujangam; Sanatanweb Sharada Bhujangam.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the traditional story behind the Vishnu Bhujanga Prayata Stotram's composition?
seoFaqs.answer: Hagiographies of Adi Shankara relate that he composed the Vishnu Bhujangam at his mother Aryamba's deathbed. He had earlier composed Shiva Bhujanga Prayata Stotram for her salvation, but his mother, frightened by the appearance of Shiva's terrible-looking ganas (Bhutas and Pishachas) coming to escort her, asked her son for a gentler form. Shankara then composed the Vishnu Bhujangam, and upon its recitation, the gentle Vishnu-parshadas riding peacocks and bearing flowers came to receive her soul peacefully. The composition is therefore loved by devotees as the Acharya's gift for ensuring a peaceful death and post-mortem journey under Vishnu's protection. (Sources: Hithokthi; Hindu-Blog Subramanya Bhujangam Story; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Vishnu Bhujangam differ from Vishnu Sahasranama and Vishnu Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The three texts serve overlapping but distinct purposes. Vishnu Sahasranama lists 1000 names of Vishnu in the Anushtubh meter (8 syllables per line) and is from the Mahabharata, used for comprehensive divine invocation across Smarta households. Vishnu Stotram is a generic category of shorter praise-hymns from various Puranic sources. Vishnu Bhujangam is a sustained 12-syllable meter composition by a single named author (Adi Shankara) intended for emotive bhakti and especially for the moment of death (antakale), since its melodic flow and Vaishnava imagery of soothing presence is held to ease the journey of the soul. Many Smarta households recite the Sahasranama daily and reserve the Bhujangam for end-of-life vigil. (Sources: Hindupedia; Hithokthi; Hindupedia Devi Bhujanga Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: What images of Vishnu does the Bhujangam emphasise?
seoFaqs.answer: Drawing on the gentler register required by his mother's request, Shankara highlights Vishnu's saumya (peaceful) iconography: the four arms holding conch (Panchajanya), discus (Sudarshana), mace (Kaumodaki) and lotus, the chest marked with Srivatsa and Kaustubha, the yellow pitambara, the consort Lakshmi seated on the chest, the snake-bed of Adi Shesha, the vehicle Garuda, and the lotus feet adored by Brahma. The hymn moves from feet upward to crown across its verses, similar to the Padadi-Keshanta structure, and ends with a refrain offering the devotee's surrender to these divine feet at the moment of death. (Sources: Hindupedia; Hithokthi; OmShivam Bhakti Vishnu.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Vishnu Bhujangam be recited for maximum efficacy?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is most powerful when recited at the bedside of a dying devotee in the final hours, ideally with a Tulsi mala in the chanter's hand and gangajala or Tulsi-tirtha placed in the mouth of the dying person. For daily recitation, the recommended times are morning Brahmamuhurta and the evening sandhya, particularly on Ekadashi, Dvadashi, Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Vishnu's nakshatra (Shravana) days, and Vishnu's avatar jayantis. The flowing meter is suited to musical chanting; reciters often follow the Vishnu Bhujangam with the Padmavati Ashtottara or Lakshmi Ashtottara to honour the consort. The full text takes approximately twelve to fifteen minutes at a measured pace. (Sources: Hithokthi; Hindupedia; Hindu-Blog Subramanya Bhujangam.)

seoFaqs.question: Are there other Bhujanga compositions that pair with Vishnu Bhujangam?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. Within Adi Shankara's corpus, the Bhujanga Prayata family includes Shiva Bhujangam (for Shaiva devotees), Devi Bhujangam (for Shakta devotees, also called Anandalahari in some recensions), Subramanya Bhujangam (composed at Tiruchendur for healing of Shankara's eye affliction), Sharada Bhujangam (composed at Sringeri for Goddess Sharada), and Ganesha Bhujangam. Many Smarta panchayatana households recite the appropriate Bhujangam alongside Vishnu Bhujangam in a sequence reflecting the day of the week or family ishta-devata. The shared meter and structure across these compositions creates a unified bhakti-experience even when invoking different deities, illustrating Shankara's overarching panchayatana inclusivism. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi Bhujanga; Greenmesg Subramanya Bhujangam; Sanatanweb Sharada Bhujangam.)
